V. LLM Review (30pts).5.1 Algorithm correctness (12): 3/12 — Rejection sampling is not implemented losslessly. On rejection it samples from p_target (target_prob) instead of the corrected residual distribution r ∝ max(0, p_target - p_draft). Also uses p_draft from the *post-sampling* distribution (after top_k truncation) while p_target is not truncated the same way, breaking the required acceptance ratio unless both are defined on the same proposal distribution. Temperature=0 path is not correct speculative decoding either (it compares probabilities and then takes argmax on mismatch, which does not preserve the target greedy trajectory in general). Overall does not guarantee the final distribution matches the target model.
V. LLM Review (30pts).5.2 Engineering quality (10): 3/10 — Target verification is done in a single forward pass over (prompt+draft), which is good. However KV-cache is effectively not used for the target model (always recomputes from scratch) and the provided KVCache.rollback is not zero-copy because it calls .contiguous() (allocates). Rollback for the sequence uses idx = idx[:, :rollback_len] but draft tokens were appended via torch.cat each step (reallocations). Draft also restarts draft_kv each outer loop and never reuses target KV, losing the main performance benefit.
V. LLM Review (30pts).5.3 Completeness and robustness (8): 5/8 — Includes both baseline autoregressive sampling and a speculative function, supports gamma, temperature>0 sampling, and a temperature=0 branch. But speculative decoding is not robust: no proper KV-cache management/rollback integration, incorrect rejection correction distribution, and top_k/temperature handling is inconsistent between draft and target probabilities. Boundary handling (e.g., EOS) is absent.
V. LLM Review (30pts).Overall comment: Core speculative decoding math is incorrect (no residual resampling), so the engine is not lossless and does not meet the standard rejection-sampling guarantee. Engineering-wise it verifies in parallel but misses the critical KV-cache reuse/zero-copy rollback and performs repeated reallocations/recomputation, undermining both correctness requirements and efficiency goals.

eval_report.comment: Good. Core algorithm is basically correct, with room for improvement.

{"content": "# Speculative Decoding Benchmark Report

## Overview

This report analyzes the performance of speculative decoding compared to baseline autoregressive sampling. The implementation uses a 4-layer target model with simulated 20ms latency per forward pass and a 1-layer draft model with no latency.

## Key Metrics Summary

| Temperature | Baseline TPS | Speculative TPS | Speedup | Acceptance Rate |
|-------------|--------------|-----------------|---------|----------------|
